channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Flytip AGAIN, 24th March
item.description:
Can you sort this please ? Thankyou x

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 64, Cotmandene Crescent, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ongoing private drive way repair, has made a man to decide to blow the entrapped /blocked dust in a leaf blower, to blow it out on the public road, not spraying water, so all cars got covered in dust. I have made an observation to the worker about it., 24th March
item.description:
Ongoing private drive way repair, has made a man to decide to blow the entrapped /blocked dust in the leaf blower, to blow it out on the public road, not spraying water while doing so, and all cars got covered in dust. I have made an observation to the worker about it. It’s okay to do any work within common sense, but not to blow the rubbish, waste and dust on the road, so cars would get a good layer of dust. And other residents would have to breathe dust but not air. Property causing the issue: 30 Carlton Road, Sidcup. Time around: 9:40 AM

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beavers Lodge, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Height Barrier Removed, 24th March
item.description:
I notice the height barrier has been removed , i presume for refuse vehicles to gain access to recyling banks , this means there may be an issue with travellers looking for somewhere to park up along with the issue that is learner drivers using the car park for practice
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lansdowne Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish and paint spilt on pavement and road, 24th March
item.description:
Spilt paint in the road and rubbish along the road needs to be cleaned away.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Totnes Road, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Environmental vandalism, 24th March
item.description:
Newly planted space at junction of Reighton Road and Brooke Road- someone has (clearly) stolen all the plants. Noticed this last night.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brooke Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Oil Spillage, 24th March
item.description:
Oil spillage in road from Viking way to churchmanorway needs urgent attention
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Church Manorway, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned overflowing bin, 24th March
item.description:
This abandoned wheely bin with no lid has been abandoned on a dead spot of Ceres road which is a fly tipping hotspot. It doesn't seem to belong to any house and is being used as a public bin but never gets emptied. So rubbish is overflowing and gets blown down the street.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kashgar Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping hotspot, late-night chaos, and unsafe parking abuse., 24th March
item.description:
I’m starting to wonder if this street has been unofficially reclassified as a dumping ground and night-time car park because that’s exactly how it’s being treated. There is a growing pile of fly-tipped rubbish here. Bags, loose waste, and random items that clearly didn’t just appear by accident. This is deliberate dumping and it’s becoming a regular occurrence. On top of that, the area is constantly left covered in litter. McDonald’s packaging, empty bottles, and general rubbish scattered around after nights out. It feels like people treat this as an extension of the pub rather than a residential street where families actually live. The situation escalates every Friday night and Saturday night. People come specifically to park here to go to the nearby Latin pub, taking up already limited resident parking spaces. Then around 2–3am they return making excessive noise, shouting, blasting music, slamming doors, and leaving even more rubbish behind. More concerningly, many of them appear to be driving off while intoxicated. This is not just anti-social behaviour, it’s dangerous and puts residents at real risk. So currently we have: Fly-tipping during the day Littering with fast food and bottles Loss of resident parking due to non-residents Late-night noise disturbance on Fridays and Saturdays Suspected drink driving If this continues, we may as well give the rubbish its own postcode and install traffic lights for the 3am exit queue. Please can this be addressed urgently, both clearing the waste and considering enforcement such as parking controls, monitoring, or patrols. Residents are paying for permits and deserve basic safety, cleanliness, and the ability to sleep at night. Thank you for your help on behalf of residents who would really like their street back.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Montrell Road, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Contractors mess, 24th March
item.description:
Contractors have been working on the corner of Tile Kiln Lane and Baldwin’s Park. They now seem to have finished, but the paving slabs are all broken, there is mud and grit left all over the pavement. This is definitely not the way that this should have been left, it should have been swept clean, the broken slabs replaced and hosed down and left clean and tidy

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tile Kiln Lane, Coldblow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken bench, 24th March
item.description:
Bench is dangerous. Back support has broke in gardens.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Trafalgar Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damage manhole cover, 24th March
item.description:
The manhole cover surrounding has broken away so the manhole cover when my blind friend stepped on it collapsed trapping his foot and I had to release him. This needs urgent repairs.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 30, Marion Crescent, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St. Martin's Road, Stockwell, Greater London

item.title: Fly tipping, 24th March
item.description:
Mattress and shopping trolley dumped. Can we have a no fly-tipping sign? This area is affected alot

item.title: General household fly tip item. Carpet, MDF. Single also there but possibly moved to other side of street, 24th March

item.title: 98 Bust stop J, 24th March
item.description:
Subject: Safety Concern at Bus Stop 98 J Dear Camden Council, I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to bring to your attention a safety concern at bus stop 98 J. There is a stone that has been installed by the council, which is currently hidden in the bushes. This stone is difficult to see, and it has caused some people to accidentally hit it. I have attached an image for your reference. I believe it would be beneficial to either make the stone more visible or consider relocating it to ensure the safety of pedestrians and bus users. Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your response. Best regards, Meroz Diniz 07985154815
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Red Lion Square, Holborn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
